---
title: "live_distributions"
author: "Sahifa"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
source("../scripts/ggprob.R")
```

```{r}
# d function gives you the prob of a point x in the binom(size, prob) distribution

# Example: flip a coin 10 times

# Find the prob of getting 4 heads

dbinom(4, 10, 0.5)

dbinom(0:10, 10, 0.5)

sum(dbinom(0:10, 10, 0.5))
```


```{r}
# p function, by default, gives the prob to the left of the point q (P(X ≤ q))

# find the prob of getting atmost 4 heads (P(X ≤ 4))

pbinom(4, 10, 0.5)

# find the prob of getting atleast 4 heads (P(X ≥ 4))

sum(dbinom(4:10, 10, 0.5))
1 - sum(dbinom(0:3, 10 , 0.5))

pbinom(3, 10, 0.5, lower.tail = F)
1 - pbinom(3, 10, 0.5)

pbinom(4, 10, 0.5, lower.tail = F)
```

```{r}
# q function (inverse of the p function) gives a point q which covers a given prob `p`

# Median of the 10 coin flips, p = 0.5 0, 1, 2,, .... , 11

qbinom(0.5, 10, 0.5)
qbinom(0.5, 10, 0.5, lower.tail = F)

pbinom(5, 10, 0.5)
pbinom(4, 10, 0.5, lower.tail = F)
pbinom(4, 10, 0.5)

# 25th percentile, 75th percentile, 90th percentile

qbinom(pbinom(5, 10, 0.5), 10, 0.5) # p and q are inverses of each other
```

```{r}
rbinom(10, 10, 0.5)
```

```{r}
gbinom(10, 0.5) + geom_binom_density(10, 0.5, a = 3, b = 4 , color = "red")
```

# Normal Distribution


N(10, 0.2) (mean, sd)

```{r}
# find the density of getting 6
dnorm(6, 10, 0.2)
dnorm(6.1, 10, 0.2)
dnorm(6.11, 10, 0.2)
dnorm(6.000001, 10, 0.2)
```

```{r}
pnorm(6, 10, 0.2)
pnorm(6, 10, 0.2, lower.tail = F)
1 - pnorm(6, 10, 0.2)
1 - pnorm(5, 10, 0.2) # strict and non-strict inequalities are the same 
```
```{r}
qnorm(0.9, 10, 0.2)
pnorm(10.25631, 10, 0.2)
```
